name: @orrgal1/devloop
description: Thin, repo-agnostic dev-loop skills for stacked-PR work. /restack brings a PR's base into its branch (merge by default, --rebase opt-in), syncing the base with upstream first. /restack-all does the same across an entire stack bottom-up. /deslop scans a PR diff and strips AI slop — verbose obvious comments and over-complex local code — reusing the built-in /simplify pass. Infers PR topology from GitHub by default, falls back to raw git analysis. /request-review ranks the most relevant peer reviewer for a PR by signal precedence (same-stack reviewers > reviewers of the author's work > people the author reviews > code-area/CODEOWNERS) and, gated, marks ready + requests them; chain-blind, so forge consumes it via its capability map. /find-blocker classifies whether a PR is held by an external blocker (red/behind base PR, infra incident, sibling PR) across gh/Slack/infra and emits a neutral, waitable condition spec — discovery only, also chain-blind. /ci-green drives a GitHub PR's CI to green — a bounded fix-to-green loop (mergeability gate + three-probe snapshot → ci-check; minimal fix + commit + push → ci-fix) with an optional --until-merge continuous monitor, plus generic extension points (--protect paths a fix must never touch, --state for loop memory, --on-green post-hook); chain-blind, so forge wraps it for chain policy. /review runs a multi-channel PR review — parallel lens fan-out (the lens-reviewer agent + a lens pool) plus always-on built-in /code-review and /security-review channels — and aggregates findings into one ranked verdict; channels/lenses/personas are extensible and a caller layers context via --context / --context-lens-dir (forge supplies its chain lenses that way). /review-watch arms a persistent monitor over a PR that fires a caller-supplied --on-trigger handler on each new review-like event (review, comment, bot/external-tool review) and re-arms until stopped — single-flight, cursor-tracked, manual lifecycle; chain-blind, so forge wraps it to dispatch its review consumer or contract router. No dependency on other plugins.
